verses.text: 'A good name is rather to be chosen than great riches'; rich and poor meet, for the LORD made them all; the prudent foresees evil and hides, but the simple are punished; 'by humility and the fear of the LORD are riches, and honour, and life.' Reputation outranks wealth. Humility and reverence bring true reward.

Reputation outranks wealth. Humility and reverence bring true reward.

Thorns and snares lie in the froward's way; 'train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it'; the borrower is servant to the lender. Early training shapes a life. Debt makes a man a servant.

He that sows iniquity reaps vanity; the bountiful eye is blessed for giving to the poor; 'cast out the scorner, and contention shall go out.' Sowing has its harvest. Remove the mocker, and strife departs with him.

He that loves a pure heart and gracious lips has the king for his friend; the LORD preserves knowledge but overthrows the transgressor; the slothful invents excuses, fearing a lion in the streets. Grace wins favour. Laziness hides behind imagined dangers.

The mouth of strange women is a deep pit; foolishness bound in a child is driven out by the rod; he that oppresses the poor to enrich himself shall come to want. Folly needs correction. Gain wrung from the poor leads only to loss.

Now begin the 'words of the wise': bow your ear and apply your heart to knowledge, for it is pleasant to keep them, 'that thy trust may be in the LORD.' Wisdom is to be treasured within. Its aim is to fix our trust on God.

Have I not written excellent things, that you may answer truly to those who send you? 'Rob not the poor, because he is poor,' for the LORD will plead their cause and spoil those who spoil them. God defends the weak. To prey on the poor is to provoke their Defender.

Make no friendship with an angry man, lest you learn his ways and snare your soul; be not among those who strike hands in surety for debts. Bad company is catching. Avoid rash pledges that can ruin you.

If you have nothing to pay, why should your bed be taken from under you? Remove not the ancient landmark; 'seest thou a man diligent in his business? he shall stand before kings.' Diligence is honoured. The skilled and faithful rise to high places.
